props_visible: Woven gold round placemats (same as long table)
Kilim rug visible under/near table (red geometric pattern)
notable_visual_elements: Round table = Saudi family/group dining equality (everyone equidistant from center)
Full menu spread across one table — 'what you can order' showcasing approach
Kilim rug under table grounds the scene in Najdi craft tradition
Empty seats with placemats invite viewer to imagine themselves at the table
Gold teapot/dallah visible = hospitality marker even in food-focused shot

hospitality_cues: Abundant round-table spread = Saudi generosity principle — guests should never want for more
Round table format = communal equality in dining
Dallah visible even in food spread = hospitality marker cannot be omitted
cultural_notes.free_notes: The menu mix here is deliberate: French toast + flatbread + salad + skillet dish = a blended menu that serves both traditional (flatbread, skillet) and modern brunch (French toast, lemonade) preferences in one table. The kilim rug grounds it all. This is the 'welcome everyone' table — showing the menu is broad enough that any guest finds their comfort food, while the setting anchors it to Najdi identity.

pattern_matches.notes: Full table spread with multiple dishes — menu showcase in natural setting
Round table in full environmental context — kilim, curtains, warm light
